detaileddescription.para: Get movie fps rate

This function is provided for backward compatibility. It cannot deal with multiple video tracks. In LibVLC versions prior to 3.0, it would also fail if the file format did not convey the frame rate explicitly.

simplesect.para: frames per second (fps) for this playing movie, or 0 if unspecified

detaileddescription.para:
end bug Obsolète
Use libvlc_media_player_set_nsobject() instead
Description for titles
string argument
Marq options definition
Navigation mode
Enumeration of values used to set position (e.g. of video title).
Enumeration of teletext keys than can be passed via libvlc_video_set_teletext()
Description for video, audio tracks and subtitles. It contains id, name (description string) and pointer to next record.
Description for chapters
Description for audio output. It contains name, description and pointer to next record.
Description for audio output device.
Marq options definition
Navigation mode
Enumeration of values used to set position (e.g. of video title).
Enumeration of teletext keys than can be passed via libvlc_video_set_teletext()
Opaque equalizer handle.
Equalizer settings can be applied to a media player.
Callback prototype to allocate and lock a picture buffer.

Whenever a new video frame needs to be decoded, the lock callback is invoked. Depending on the video chroma, one or three pixel planes of adequate dimensions must be returned via the second parameter. Those planes must be aligned on 32-bytes boundaries.

parameterdescription.para: private pointer as passed to libvlc_video_set_callbacks() [IN]
start address of the pixel planes (LibVLC allocates the array of void pointers, this callback must initialize the array) [OUT]

simplesect.para: a private pointer for the display and unlock callbacks to identify the picture buffers

detaileddescription.para: Callback prototype to unlock a picture buffer.

When the video frame decoding is complete, the unlock callback is invoked. This callback might not be needed at all. It is only an indication that the application can now read the pixel values if it needs to.

simplesect.para: A picture buffer is unlocked after the picture is decoded, but before the picture is displayed.

parameterdescription.para: private pointer as passed to libvlc_video_set_callbacks() [IN]
private pointer returned from the libvlc_video_lock_cb callback [IN]
pixel planes as defined by the libvlc_video_lock_cb callback (this parameter is only for convenience) [IN]

detaileddescription.para: Callback prototype to display a picture.
When the video frame needs to be shown, as determined by the media playback clock, the display callback is invoked.

parameterdescription.para: private pointer as passed to libvlc_video_set_callbacks() [IN]
private pointer returned from the libvlc_video_lock_cb callback [IN]

memberdef.definition: typedef unsigned(* libvlc_video_format_cb) (void **opaque, char *chroma, unsigned *width, unsigned *height, unsigned *pitches, unsigned *lines)
memberdef.name: libvlc_video_format_cb

detaileddescription.para: Callback prototype to configure picture buffers format. This callback gets the format of the video as output by the video decoder and the chain of video filters (if any). It can opt to change any parameter as it needs. In that case, LibVLC will attempt to convert the video format (rescaling and chroma conversion) but these operations can be CPU intensive.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the private pointer passed to libvlc_video_set_callbacks() [IN/OUT]
pointer to the 4 bytes video format identifier [IN/OUT]
table of scanline pitches in bytes for each pixel plane (the table is allocated by LibVLC) [OUT]
table of scanlines count for each plane [OUT]

simplesect.para: the number of picture buffers allocated, 0 indicates failure

For each pixels plane, the scanline pitch must be bigger than or equal to the number of bytes per pixel multiplied by the pixel width. Similarly, the number of scanlines must be bigger than of equal to the pixel height. Furthermore, we recommend that pitches and lines be multiple of 32 to not break assumptions that might be held by optimized code in the video decoders, video filters and/or video converters.

detaileddescription.para: Callback prototype to configure picture buffers format.

parameterdescription.para: private pointer as passed to libvlc_video_set_callbacks() (and possibly modified by libvlc_video_format_cb) [IN]

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* libvlc_audio_play_cb) (void *data, const void *samples, unsigned count, int64_t pts)
memberdef.argsstring: )(void *data, const void *samples, unsigned count, int64_t pts)
memberdef.name: libvlc_audio_play_cb

detaileddescription.para: Callback prototype for audio playback.
The LibVLC media player decodes and post-processes the audio signal asynchronously (in an internal thread). Whenever audio samples are ready to be queued to the output, this callback is invoked.

The number of samples provided per invocation may depend on the file format, the audio coding algorithm, the decoder plug-in, the post-processing filters and timing. Application must not assume a certain number of samples.
The exact format of audio samples is determined by libvlc_audio_set_format() or libvlc_audio_set_format_callbacks() as is the channels layout.

Note that the number of samples is per channel. For instance, if the audio track sampling rate is 48000 Hz, then 1200 samples represent 25 milliseconds of audio signal - regardless of the number of audio channels.

parameterdescription.para: data pointer as passed to libvlc_audio_set_callbacks() [IN]
pointer to a table of audio samples to play back [IN]
number of audio samples to play back

detaileddescription.para: Callback prototype for audio pause.
LibVLC invokes this callback to pause audio playback.

simplesect.para: The pause callback is never called if the audio is already paused.

parameterdescription.para: data pointer as passed to libvlc_audio_set_callbacks() [IN]
time stamp of the pause request (should be elapsed already)

detaileddescription.para: Callback prototype for audio resumption.
LibVLC invokes this callback to resume audio playback after it was previously paused.

simplesect.para: The resume callback is never called if the audio is not paused.

parameterdescription.para: data pointer as passed to libvlc_audio_set_callbacks() [IN]
time stamp of the resumption request (should be elapsed already)

detaileddescription.para: Callback prototype for audio buffer flush.
LibVLC invokes this callback if it needs to discard all pending buffers and stop playback as soon as possible. This typically occurs when the media is stopped.

parameterdescription.para: data pointer as passed to libvlc_audio_set_callbacks() [IN]

detaileddescription.para: Callback prototype for audio buffer drain.

LibVLC may invoke this callback when the decoded audio track is ending. There will be no further decoded samples for the track, but playback should nevertheless continue until all already pending buffers are rendered.

parameterdescription.para: data pointer as passed to libvlc_audio_set_callbacks() [IN]

memberdef.definition: typedef void(* libvlc_audio_set_volume_cb) (void *data, float volume, bool mute)
memberdef.name: libvlc_audio_set_volume_cb

detaileddescription.para:
Callback prototype for audio volume change.
data
data pointer as passed to libvlc_audio_set_callbacks() [IN]
volume
software volume (1. = nominal, 0. = mute)
mute
muted flag

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* libvlc_audio_setup_cb) (void **data, char *format, unsigned *rate, unsigned *channels)
memberdef.name: libvlc_audio_setup_cb

detaileddescription.para: Callback prototype to setup the audio playback.

This is called when the media player needs to create a new audio output.
opaque
pointer to the data pointer passed to libvlc_audio_set_callbacks() [IN/OUT]
format
4 bytes sample format [IN/OUT]
rate
sample rate [IN/OUT]
channels
channels count [IN/OUT]
0 on success, anything else to skip audio playback
Callback prototype for audio playback cleanup.

This is called when the media player no longer needs an audio output.
opaque
data pointer as passed to libvlc_audio_set_callbacks() [IN]
Create an empty Media Player object

parameterdescription.para: the libvlc instance in which the Media Player should be created.

simplesect.para: a new media player object, or NULL on error.

detaileddescription.para: Create a Media Player object from a Media

parameterdescription.para: the media. Afterwards the p_md can be safely destroyed.

simplesect.para: a new media player object, or NULL on error.

detaileddescription.para: Release a media_player after use Decrement the reference count of a media player object. If the reference count is 0, then libvlc_media_player_release() will release the media player object. If the media player object has been released, then it should not be used again.
Retain a reference to a media player object. Use libvlc_media_player_release() to decrement reference count.
Set the media that will be used by the media_player. If any, previous md will be released.

parameterdescription.para: the Media. Afterwards the p_md can be safely destroyed.

detaileddescription.para: Get the media used by the media_player.

simplesect.para: the media associated with p_mi, or NULL if no media is associated

detaileddescription.para: Get the Event Manager from which the media player send event.

simplesect.para: the event manager associated with p_mi

detaileddescription.para: is_playing

p_mi
the Media Player
1 if the media player is playing, 0 otherwise
\libvlc_return_bool
Play

simplesect.para: 0 if playback started (and was already started), or -1 on error.

detaileddescription.para: Pause or resume (no effect if there is no media)

parameterdescription.para: play/resume if zero, pause if non-zero

simplesect.para: LibVLC 1.1.1 or later

detaileddescription.para: Toggle pause (no effect if there is no media)
Stop (no effect if there is no media)
Set a renderer to the media player

simplesect.para: must be called before the first call of libvlc_media_player_play() to take effect.
0 on success, -1 on error.
LibVLC 3.0.0 or later

memberdef.argsstring: (libvlc_media_player_t *mp, libvlc_video_lock_cb lock, libvlc_video_unlock_cb unlock, libvlc_video_display_cb display, void *opaque)
memberdef.name: libvlc_video_set_callbacks

detaileddescription.para: Set callbacks and private data to render decoded video to a custom area in memory. Use libvlc_video_set_format() or libvlc_video_set_format_callbacks() to configure the decoded format.

Rendering video into custom memory buffers is considerably less efficient than rendering in a custom window as normal.
For optimal perfomances, VLC media player renders into a custom window, and does not use this function and associated callbacks. It is highly recommended that other LibVLC-based application do likewise. To embed video in a window, use libvlc_media_player_set_xid() or equivalent depending on the operating system.
If window embedding does not fit the application use case, then a custom LibVLC video output display plugin is required to maintain optimal video rendering performances.

The following limitations affect performance:
Hardware video decoding acceleration will either be disabled completely, or require (relatively slow) copy from video/DSP memory to main memory.
Sub-pictures (subtitles, on-screen display, etc.) must be blent into the main picture by the CPU instead of the GPU.
Depending on the video format, pixel format conversion, picture scaling, cropping and/or picture re-orientation, must be performed by the CPU instead of the GPU.
Memory copying is required between LibVLC reference picture buffers and application buffers (between lock and unlock callbacks).

parameterdescription.para: callback to lock video memory (must not be NULL)
callback to unlock video memory (or NULL if not needed)
callback to display video (or NULL if not needed)
private pointer for the three callbacks (as first parameter)

simplesect.para: LibVLC 1.1.1 or later

memberdef.argsstring: (libvlc_media_player_t *mp, const char *chroma, unsigned width, unsigned height, unsigned pitch)
memberdef.name: libvlc_video_set_format

detaileddescription.para: Set decoded video chroma and dimensions. This only works in combination with libvlc_video_set_callbacks(), and is mutually exclusive with libvlc_video_set_format_callbacks().

parameterdescription.para: a four-characters string identifying the chroma (e.g. "RV32" or "YUYV")

simplesect.para: LibVLC 1.1.1 or later

xrefdescription.para: All pixel planes are expected to have the same pitch. To use the YCbCr color space with chrominance subsampling, consider using libvlc_video_set_format_callbacks() instead.

detaileddescription.para: Set decoded video chroma and dimensions. This only works in combination with libvlc_video_set_callbacks().

parameterdescription.para: callback to select the video format (cannot be NULL)
callback to release any allocated resources (or NULL)

simplesect.para: LibVLC 2.0.0 or later

detaileddescription.para: Set the NSView handler where the media player should render its video output.
Use the vout called "macosx".
The drawable is an NSObject that follow the VLCOpenGLVideoViewEmbedding protocol:
Or it can be an NSView object.
You can find a live example in VLCVideoView in VLCKit.framework.

parameterdescription.para: the drawable that is either an NSView or an object following the VLCOpenGLVideoViewEmbedding protocol.

detaileddescription.para: Get the NSView handler previously set with libvlc_media_player_set_nsobject().

simplesect.para: the NSView handler or 0 if none where set

detaileddescription.para: Set an X Window System drawable where the media player should render its video output. The call takes effect when the playback starts. If it is already started, it might need to be stopped before changes apply. If LibVLC was built without X11 output support, then this function has no effects.

By default, LibVLC will capture input events on the video rendering area. Use libvlc_video_set_mouse_input() and libvlc_video_set_key_input() to disable that and deliver events to the parent window / to the application instead. By design, the X11 protocol delivers input events to only one recipient.

simplesect.para: The application must call the XInitThreads() function from Xlib before libvlc_new(), and before any call to XOpenDisplay() directly or via any other library. Failure to call XInitThreads() will seriously impede LibVLC performance. Calling XOpenDisplay() before XInitThreads() will eventually crash the process. That is a limitation of Xlib.

The specified identifier must correspond to an existing Input/Output class X11 window. Pixmaps are not currently supported. The default X11 server is assumed, i.e. that specified in the DISPLAY environment variable.

LibVLC can deal with invalid X11 handle errors, however some display drivers (EGL, GLX, VA and/or VDPAU) can unfortunately not. Thus the window handle must remain valid until playback is stopped, otherwise the process may abort or crash.

xrefdescription.para: No more than one window handle per media player instance can be specified. If the media has multiple simultaneously active video tracks, extra tracks will be rendered into external windows beyond the control of the application.

detaileddescription.para: Get the X Window System window identifier previously set with libvlc_media_player_set_xwindow(). Note that this will return the identifier even if VLC is not currently using it (for instance if it is playing an audio-only input).

simplesect.para: an X window ID, or 0 if none where set.

detaileddescription.para: Set a Win32/Win64 API window handle (HWND) where the media player should render its video output. If LibVLC was built without Win32/Win64 API output support, then this has no effects.

simplesect.para: the HWND must have the WS_CLIPCHILDREN set in its style.

detaileddescription.para: Get the Windows API window handle (HWND) previously set with libvlc_media_player_set_hwnd(). The handle will be returned even if LibVLC is not currently outputting any video to it.

simplesect.para: a window handle or NULL if there are none.

detaileddescription.para: Set the android context.

simplesect.para: LibVLC 3.0.0 and later.

parameterdescription.para: org.videolan.libvlc.AWindow jobject owned by the org.videolan.libvlc.MediaPlayer class from the libvlc-android project.

detaileddescription.para: Set the EFL Evas Object.

simplesect.para: LibVLC 3.0.0 and later.
-1 if an error was detected, 0 otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (libvlc_media_player_t *mp, libvlc_audio_play_cb play, libvlc_audio_pause_cb pause, libvlc_audio_resume_cb resume, libvlc_audio_flush_cb flush, libvlc_audio_drain_cb drain, void *opaque)
memberdef.name: libvlc_audio_set_callbacks

detaileddescription.para: Sets callbacks and private data for decoded audio.
Use libvlc_audio_set_format() or libvlc_audio_set_format_callbacks() to configure the decoded audio format.

simplesect.para: The audio callbacks override any other audio output mechanism. If the callbacks are set, LibVLC will not output audio in any way.

parameterdescription.para: callback to play audio samples (must not be NULL)
callback to pause playback (or NULL to ignore)
callback to resume playback (or NULL to ignore)
callback to flush audio buffers (or NULL to ignore)
callback to drain audio buffers (or NULL to ignore)
private pointer for the audio callbacks (as first parameter)

simplesect.para: LibVLC 2.0.0 or later

detaileddescription.para: Set callbacks and private data for decoded audio. This only works in combination with libvlc_audio_set_callbacks(). Use libvlc_audio_set_format() or libvlc_audio_set_format_callbacks() to configure the decoded audio format.

parameterdescription.para: callback to apply audio volume, or NULL to apply volume in software

simplesect.para: LibVLC 2.0.0 or later

detaileddescription.para: Sets decoded audio format via callbacks.
This only works in combination with libvlc_audio_set_callbacks().

parameterdescription.para: callback to select the audio format (cannot be NULL)
callback to release any allocated resources (or NULL)

simplesect.para: LibVLC 2.0.0 or later

memberdef.argsstring: (libvlc_media_player_t *mp, const char *format, unsigned rate, unsigned channels)
memberdef.name: libvlc_audio_set_format

detaileddescription.para: Sets a fixed decoded audio format.
This only works in combination with libvlc_audio_set_callbacks(), and is mutually exclusive with libvlc_audio_set_format_callbacks().

parameterdescription.para: a four-characters string identifying the sample format (e.g. "S16N" or "f32l")

simplesect.para: LibVLC 2.0.0 or later

detaileddescription.para:
Bogue
This might go away ... to be replaced by a broader system Get the current movie length (in ms).

simplesect.para: the movie length (in ms), or -1 if there is no media.

detaileddescription.para: Get the current movie time (in ms).

simplesect.para: the movie time (in ms), or -1 if there is no media.

detaileddescription.para: Set the movie time (in ms). This has no effect if no media is being played. Not all formats and protocols support this.
Get movie position as percentage between 0.0 and 1.0.

simplesect.para: movie position, or -1. in case of error

detaileddescription.para: Set movie position as percentage between 0.0 and 1.0. This has no effect if playback is not enabled. This might not work depending on the underlying input format and protocol.
Set movie chapter (if applicable).
Get movie chapter.

simplesect.para: chapter number currently playing, or -1 if there is no media.

detaileddescription.para: Get movie chapter count

simplesect.para: number of chapters in movie, or -1.

detaileddescription.para: Is the player able to play

p_mi
the Media Player
boolean
\libvlc_return_bool
Get title chapter count

simplesect.para: number of chapters in title, or -1

detaileddescription.para: Set movie title
Get movie title

simplesect.para: title number currently playing, or -1

detaileddescription.para: Get movie title count

simplesect.para: title number count, or -1

detaileddescription.para: Set previous chapter (if applicable)
Set next chapter (if applicable)

Get the requested movie play rate.
Depending on the underlying media, the requested rate may be different from the real playback rate.
p_mi
the Media Player
movie play rate
Set movie play rate

simplesect.para: -1 if an error was detected, 0 otherwise (but even then, it might not actually work depending on the underlying media protocol)

detaileddescription.para: Get current movie state

simplesect.para: the current state of the media player (playing, paused, ...)

detaileddescription.para: How many video outputs does this media player have?

simplesect.para: the number of video outputs

detaileddescription.para: Is this media player seekable?

p_mi
the media player
true if the media player can seek
\libvlc_return_bool
Can this media player be paused?

p_mi
the media player
true if the media player can pause
\libvlc_return_bool
Check if the current program is scrambled

p_mi
the media player
true if the current program is scrambled
\libvlc_return_bool
LibVLC 2.2.0 or later
Display the next frame (if supported)
Navigate through DVD Menu

simplesect.para: libVLC 2.0.0 or later

detaileddescription.para: Set if, and how, the video title will be shown when media is played.

parameterdescription.para: position at which to display the title, or libvlc_position_disable to prevent the title from being displayed
title display timeout in milliseconds (ignored if libvlc_position_disable)

simplesect.para: libVLC 2.1.0 or later

memberdef.argsstring: (libvlc_media_player_t *p_mi, libvlc_media_slave_type_t i_type, const char *psz_uri, bool b_select)
memberdef.name: libvlc_media_player_add_slave

detaileddescription.para: Add a slave to the current media player.

simplesect.para: If the player is playing, the slave will be added directly. This call will also update the slave list of the attached libvlc_media_t.
LibVLC 3.0.0 and later.

parameterdescription.para: Uri of the slave (should contain a valid scheme).
True if this slave should be selected when it's loaded

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 on error.

detaileddescription.para: Release (free) libvlc_track_description_t
A LibVLC media player plays one media (usually in a custom drawable).
